result.markdown:
---
title: "Quality Control & Filtering"
subtitle: "Step 2: Filtering low-quality cells and genes"
sidebar: scrnaseq
---

## Overview

Quality control is essential for removing low-quality cells (damaged, dying, or doublets) that can confound downstream analysis.

## Load Data into Seurat


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code  code-fold="false"}
library(Seurat)
library(tidyverse)

# Read 10x Cell Ranger output
data <- Read10X("sample_1/outs/filtered_feature_bc_matrix/")

# Create Seurat object
seurat_obj <- CreateSeuratObject(
  counts = data,
  project = "MyProject",
  min.cells = 3,      # Genes detected in at least 3 cells
  min.features = 200  # Cells with at least 200 genes
)
```
:::


## Calculate QC Metrics


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code  code-fold="false"}
# Calculate mitochondrial percentage
seurat_obj[["percent.mt"]] <- PercentageFeatureSet(seurat_obj, pattern = "^MT-")

# Calculate ribosomal percentage
seurat_obj[["percent.ribo"]] <- PercentageFeatureSet(seurat_obj, pattern = "^RP[SL]")

# Calculate complexity (log10 genes per UMI)
seurat_obj[["log10GenesPerUMI"]] <- log10(seurat_obj$nFeature_RNA) / log10(seurat_obj$nCount_RNA)
```
:::


## Visualize QC Metrics


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
# Violin plots
VlnPlot(seurat_obj, 
        features = c("nFeature_RNA", "nCount_RNA", "percent.mt"),
        ncol = 3,
        pt.size = 0)

# Scatter plots
plot1 <- FeatureScatter(seurat_obj, feature1 = "nCount_RNA", feature2 = "percent.mt")
plot2 <- FeatureScatter(seurat_obj, feature1 = "nCount_RNA", feature2 = "nFeature_RNA")
plot1 + plot2
```
:::


### QC Metric Distributions


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
# Detailed distributions
seurat_obj@meta.data |>
  pivot_longer(cols = c(nCount_RNA, nFeature_RNA, percent.mt),
               names_to = "metric", values_to = "value") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = value)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, fill = "#3498db", color = "white") +
  facet_wrap(~metric, scales = "free") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "QC Metric Distributions")
```
:::


## Define Filtering Thresholds

::: {.callout-note}
## Threshold Guidelines

Thresholds should be determined empirically for each dataset:

- **nFeature_RNA**: 200-5000 (adjust based on distribution)
- **nCount_RNA**: 500-50000 (adjust based on distribution)  
- **percent.mt**: <10-20% (depends on tissue type)
:::


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code  code-fold="false"}
# Define thresholds
min_genes <- 200
max_genes <- 5000
min_counts <- 500
max_mt <- 15

# Apply filters
seurat_obj <- subset(seurat_obj,
                     subset = nFeature_RNA > min_genes &
                              nFeature_RNA < max_genes &
                              nCount_RNA > min_counts &
                              percent.mt < max_mt)

# Report filtering results
message(sprintf("Cells before filtering: %d", ncol(data)))
message(sprintf("Cells after filtering: %d", ncol(seurat_obj)))
```
:::


## Doublet Detection

Doublets (two cells in one droplet) can create artificial intermediate populations.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code  code-fold="false"}
# Using DoubletFinder
library(DoubletFinder)

# Pre-process for DoubletFinder
seurat_obj <- NormalizeData(seurat_obj)
seurat_obj <- FindVariableFeatures(seurat_obj)
seurat_obj <- ScaleData(seurat_obj)
seurat_obj <- RunPCA(seurat_obj)
seurat_obj <- RunUMAP(seurat_obj, dims = 1:20)
seurat_obj <- FindNeighbors(seurat_obj, dims = 1:20)
seurat_obj <- FindClusters(seurat_obj, resolution = 0.5)

# Find optimal pK
sweep.res <- paramSweep(seurat_obj, PCs = 1:20, sct = FALSE)
sweep.stats <- summarizeSweep(sweep.res, GT = FALSE)
bcmvn <- find.pK(sweep.stats)
pK_optimal <- as.numeric(as.character(bcmvn$pK[which.max(bcmvn$BCmetric)]))

# Estimate doublet rate (~0.8% per 1000 cells)
nExp_poi <- round(0.008 * ncol(seurat_obj)^2 / 10000)

# Run DoubletFinder
seurat_obj <- doubletFinder(seurat_obj, 
                            PCs = 1:20, 
                            pN = 0.25, 
                            pK = pK_optimal, 
                            nExp = nExp_poi)

# Remove doublets
seurat_obj <- subset(seurat_obj, subset = DF.classifications == "Singlet")
```
:::


## Multi-Sample Processing


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code  code-fold="false"}
# Process multiple samples
sample_dirs <- c("Sample1" = "Sample1/outs/filtered_feature_bc_matrix/",
                 "Sample2" = "Sample2/outs/filtered_feature_bc_matrix/",
                 "Sample3" = "Sample3/outs/filtered_feature_bc_matrix/")

seurat_list <- lapply(names(sample_dirs), function(sample_name) {
  data <- Read10X(sample_dirs[[sample_name]])
  obj <- CreateSeuratObject(counts = data, project = sample_name)
  obj$sample <- sample_name
  obj[["percent.mt"]] <- PercentageFeatureSet(obj, pattern = "^MT-")
  
  # Filter each sample
  obj <- subset(obj, 
                subset = nFeature_RNA > 200 & 
                         nFeature_RNA < 5000 & 
                         percent.mt < 15)
  return(obj)
})

# Merge all samples
seurat_merged <- merge(seurat_list[[1]], 
                       y = seurat_list[-1],
                       add.cell.ids = names(sample_dirs))
```
:::


## Summary Statistics


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
# Summary after filtering
summary_stats <- seurat_obj@meta.data |>
  summarise(
    n_cells = n(),
    median_genes = median(nFeature_RNA),
    median_UMIs = median(nCount_RNA),
    median_mt = median(percent.mt)
  )

summary_stats
```
:::


## Next Steps

With filtered data, proceed to [Normalization](03-normalization.qmd).
